articles.title: First Upgraded Ariane 6 Rocket Launches 36 Satellites for Amazon
articles.summary:
Arianespace has successfully completed its third Ariane 6 flight of 2026, delivering another 36 Amazon satellites to orbit aboard a new variant of the rocket that features the upgraded P160C solid-fuel boosters. The Ariane 64 rocket, which features four solid-fuel boosters, lifted off at 12:21 UTC from ELA-4 at the Guiana Space Centre. The launch […]
The post First Upgraded Ariane 6 Rocket Launches 36 Satellites for Amazon appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: Search for Hidden Cosmic Companions in Sun’s Backyard
articles.summary: Some stars have planets. Others are orbited by brown dwarfs, balls of gas too massive to be planets, but too low-mass to be stars. Astronomers love these brown dwarf-star pairs because being paired with a star helps reveal a brown dwarf’s age. Ages of astronomical objects are often hard to measure, but essential for understanding […]

articles.title: Upgraded Ariane 6 launches Amazon Leo satellites
articles.summary:
An Ariane 6 with upgraded solid rocket boosters successfully launched three dozen Amazon Leo satellites June 17 as ESA weighs options for increasing the vehicle’s launch rate.
The post Upgraded Ariane 6 launches Amazon Leo satellites appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Ariane 6 flight VA269 - full replay
articles.summary: On the 17th of June Ariane 6 flight VA269 soared to orbit from Europe's Spaceport in French Guiana. The latest generation of Europe's largest and most powerful rocket launched 36 satellites for Amazon's Leo constellation.

articles.title: 347th ESA Council: Media information session
articles.summary: Watch the replay of the media information session where ESA Director General Josef Aschbacher and outgoing ESA Council Chair Renato Krpoun provide an update on the main outcomes of the 347th ESA Council meeting, held at ESA Headquarters in Paris on 16–17 June 2026.

articles.title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
articles.summary: Looking somewhat like a swarm of bees returning to their hive, this NASA Hubble Space Telescope image released on June 12, 2026, features the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211. Galaxy clusters like MACS0329-0211 are important signposts in the story of how the structure of the universe evolved, and are the ultimate telescopic lenses, placing gravitationally lensed galaxies from the […]
